subject: Statistics
title: AI in Medical Diagnosis
description: A micro-lesson that makes AI in Medical Diagnosis usable.

hook.text: An AI reads a chest X-ray in 2 seconds and matches a radiologist's accuracy. Is the doctor obsolete?

buildup.text: Machine learning models trained on millions of images can detect tumors, fractures, and infections.

discovery.text: In some studies, AI outperforms individual doctors on specific tasks like detecting diabetic eye disease.

twist.text: AI models are only as good as their training data. Biased data produces biased diagnoses — worse for minorities.

climax.text: The future is AI assisting doctors, not replacing them. The combination outperforms either alone.

punchline.text: AI plus doctor beats AI alone and doctor alone.

quiz.question: What is the main risk of AI in medical diagnosis?
options: Models trained on biased data can produce biased diagnoses, especially for underrepresented groups
AI is always less accurate than human doctors
AI diagnoses are too expensive for hospitals
